# Daily Report — 2026-04-29

## Task Completed
Added `create_asset` tool with `alm_hardware` subclass support to `asset_tools.py`.

- New `CreateAssetParams` model covers all `alm_asset` base fields plus 14 hardware-specific fields (`cpu_count`, `cpu_core_count`, `cpu_manufacturer`, `cpu_name`, `cpu_speed`, `disk_space`, `ram`, `os`, `os_version`, `os_service_pack`, `os_domain`, `mac_address`, `ip_address`) grouped under the optional `asset_class` routing field.
- `create_asset()` POSTs to `alm_asset` by default; set `asset_class="alm_hardware"` to target the hardware subclass table.
- `_format_asset()` updated to conditionally include hardware fields when present in the record, so get/list responses for hardware assets surface those fields without polluting plain asset responses.
- `_build_create_body()` excludes the `asset_class` routing field from the POST body.
- Tool registered in `__init__.py`, `tool_utils.py`, and both package blocks in `tool_packages.yaml`.

## Test Results
- Total tests: 817 passed
- Coverage: asset_tools.py 99%, overall 90%
- All passing: yes

## Security
System-level vulnerabilities in pip/setuptools/wheel/pyjwt — no issues in project code.

- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — added `create_asset` import and `__all__` entry
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — added `CreateAssetParams` import, `create_asset_tool`, tool definition entry
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — added `create_asset` to asset management sections
- `tests/test_asset_tools.py` — added `TestCreateAsset` (11 tests), `TestFormatAssetHardware` (2 tests), 3 model tests; `FAKE_HARDWARE_ASSET` fixture
